naming_note: All names drawn from expanded name banks file 26. Cauldenmoor borderland register. No A-name clustering. Sorn appears twice (Vael Sorn, Edric Sorn) as an acknowledged common borderland surname. Marsh appears twice (Kela Marsh, Torald Marsh) as an acknowledged common borderland surname. No other surnames repeated across major figures.

aldenmeres_cross_garrison.name: Pell Drant
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.physical: Young enough to still be learning how to manage a senior commander's schedule, competent enough that Brennwatch kept him after the first month.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.disposition: Efficient, protective of Brennwatch's time, reads situations faster than his age suggests.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.secret: Has been carrying the morning's correspondence to Brennwatch for six weeks and has noticed that certain letters are opened, read, and locked away rather than filed — which is not the normal correspondence procedure. Has not asked about it. Has not told anyone. Is beginning to understand that not knowing things is sometimes the safer professional position.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.player_interaction: The formal access point to Brennwatch. His assessment of who gets a meeting is quick and influenced by how they present themselves in the garrison entrance.

aldenmeres_cross_garrison.name: Denveth Hale
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.physical: Has been managing supply chains for the garrison for nine years. Looks like someone who has been doing math in their head for nine years — a specific quality of perpetual calculation.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.disposition: Blunt about what the garrison has and does not have. Does not soften the numbers for anyone including Brennwatch.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.secret: The garrison's equipment replacement budget has been underfunded for three years. Has been filing the correct reports. The reports have been received and the budget has not changed. Has begun keeping a separate private ledger of what the garrison needs versus what it has, which he intends to present to Brennwatch when the situation requires it. The situation is closer to requiring it than he has said.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.player_interaction: The notice board outside his office carries the auxiliary garrison posting. Accessible for anyone who applies. Honest about what the work involves and what the pay is.

aldenmeres_cross_garrison.name: Rald Morcroft
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.physical: Twenty years of borderland service in his face. The kind of senior sergeant who keeps a garrison functioning between commanders and who is usually more practically capable than the officers above him know.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.disposition: Dry, sardonic about the current situation, professionally committed to the work regardless.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.secret: Has been running informal communication channels with the Grey Contracts' senior soldiers for eleven years — a practice Brennwatch knows about and tacitly approves. Through these channels he has heard that something is wrong inside the Grey Contracts without being able to identify what. Has been watching Kela Marsh's intake assessments for behavioral changes in new recruits that might indicate who the approach came from.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.player_interaction: The most experienced institutional knowledge in the garrison below Brennwatch. A player who earns his respect gets access to eleven years of borderland operational intelligence.

aldenmeres_cross_garrison.name: Wren Ashvale
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.physical: Has been treating borderland injuries for eight years. The specific efficiency of someone who has seen too many things to be distressed by any of them individually.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.disposition: Matter-of-fact about damage and repair. Has strong opinions about the garrison's equipment quality that she expresses directly to anyone who will listen and indirectly in her treatment records.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.secret: Has been treating soldiers from both the garrison and the Grey Contracts — a grey-area arrangement that Brennwatch permits because the alternative is Grey Contracts soldiers riding to the next town for treatment. Has noticed three Grey Contracts soldiers showing up for treatment of minor injuries more frequently than would be explained by patrol activity. Has not connected this to anything specific.
aldenmeres_cross_garrison.player_interaction: For players with medical backgrounds, the most immediately accessible professional contact in the garrison. Her Grey Contracts treatment thread is a secondary path to identifying behavioral changes in the company.

grey_contracts_soldiers.name: Cavan Storne
grey_contracts_soldiers.role: Senior soldier — Grey Contracts, one of Sorn's original 200
grey_contracts_soldiers.physical: Has been with Sorn since the beginning. The specific quality of someone whose loyalty is personal rather than contractual — he is loyal to Vael Sorn specifically and to the Grey Contracts because Vael Sorn built it.
grey_contracts_soldiers.disposition: Watchful, protective of Sorn's reputation, is the soldier most likely to notice something wrong inside the company before anyone else does because he has been watching the company continuously for eleven years.
grey_contracts_soldiers.secret: Has noticed Feldwick's behavior change over the past six weeks. Cannot name what changed specifically. Has been waiting for Feldwick to come to him with something because in eleven years, soldiers who change like this always eventually come to someone. Feldwick has not come to him. This worries him.
grey_contracts_soldiers.player_interaction: A player who earns sustained trust in the Grey Contracts will eventually encounter Storne's quiet concern about Feldwick. He will not say Feldwick's name. He will say enough.

grey_contracts_soldiers.name: Bren Coldwick
grey_contracts_soldiers.role: Junior Sergeant — Grey Contracts, Cravenmoor's Ford detachment commander
grey_contracts_soldiers.physical: Competent, nervous about the specific situation he is in, aware that his twelve soldiers are a symbolic presence.
grey_contracts_soldiers.disposition: Trying to project the confidence he does not feel because his soldiers need to see confidence and he is the only one they have available.
grey_contracts_soldiers.secret: Wrote a report to Sorn eight days ago requesting clarification on what 'do not engage unless directly attacked' means when the Ironmark arrives with eighty soldiers. The report has not been answered. He does not know if it was received. He is considering writing it again.
grey_contracts_soldiers.player_interaction: At Cravenmoor's Ford. A player who arrives there will encounter the detachment and its junior sergeant. His request for clarification is a thread — someone who can carry the message directly to Sorn, bypassing the report system, is more useful than the report.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Kessa Durn
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.role: Ferry operator — Durnmark River crossing, Aldenmere's Cross
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: Thirty years of ferry work have given her the build of someone who has been moving heavy things across water her entire adult life. The boat hook is always within reach.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Has watched the ford change hands four times and has operated the ferry under all of them. Her position on political situations is that boats float regardless of flags. This is not indifference — it is a considered philosophy about what continues to matter when the current administration changes.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.secret: Has been keeping a mental passenger log for thirty years. Knows who crossed, when, and carrying what — not in writing, in memory. This memory is one of the most valuable intelligence resources in the Borderlands. Three organizations have tried to access it. She declined all three with the boat hook that is always within reach.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: Operates the day ferry at standard rates. Night crossings are available at double rate through the specific process described in the spawn hook. Her passenger memory is accessible to a player who has earned genuine trust over multiple crossings — she will not share it formally, but she will mention things in conversation that a perceptive player can extract.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Morna Daelwick
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.role: Sister — Reclaimed Path temple, Aldenmere's Cross
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: Has been the actual pastoral worker at the Aldenmere's Cross temple for twenty years while a succession of Priors have done the administration and the politics. The pastoral work shows — she knows every family in the settlement by their specific circumstances.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Warm in the way of someone who has been extending warmth professionally for twenty years and has found that it was never actually professional — she genuinely cares about the people she cares for, which is everyone who comes through the temple door.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.secret: Is the contact for the underground Reclaimed Path congregation in Saltfen. Receives irregular messages through Sera Venn's network at Cravenmoor's Ford. Has been providing the Saltfen congregation with what support she can, which is less than she would like and more than she has officially authorized herself to give.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: The temple entry point. Will give one meal and one night's floor space to anyone who asks without condition. Will become a significant ally to a player who demonstrates genuine engagement with the displaced community in the South Shore.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Wren Collum
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.role: South Shore community contact — Garet Denn's operation identifier
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: Former Saltfen resident, displaced four years ago. Has the quality of someone who has been watching his surroundings with professional care ever since the day the Ironmark soldiers arrived and he was not prepared.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Cautious, perceptive, specifically good at identifying people who need the operation's services and people who might be trustworthy to the operation.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.secret: Has been identifying candidates for Garet Denn's operation for three years. Has recently identified three candidates who would be useful and has not been able to reach Garet to recommend them because the operation's communication channel to the South Shore has been quiet for a week. Does not know if this is operational caution or a problem.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: The South Shore entry point for the deserter spawn hook. Watches new arrivals from the east for ten minutes before approaching. Will approach a player who demonstrates Bleeding Ground knowledge or displacement need.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Lysa Caldren
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.note: No relation to House Caldren in Keth
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.role: Displaced Thorngate resident — South Shore, Aldenmere's Cross
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: Has been in the South Shore for four years. Has built a life here that is functional and that is not what she had. Both of these things are true simultaneously.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Has the specific knowledge of someone who was present for Thorngate's absorption and watched it happen across four years. Will share this knowledge with anyone who asks the right way — not because she wants to but because she has decided that people who are going to face what Cravenmoor's Ford is going to face deserve to know what comes after.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.what_she_knows: The first six months of Ironmark administration in Thorngate. Who left, who stayed, who thought they were accommodating and who was actually accommodating and what the difference looked like from the inside. The specific mechanics of how the Scorched Covenant replaced the Reclaimed Path in practice — not the official process but what it felt like to the families who lived it.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: Accessible in the South Shore. The most accurate firsthand account of Ironmark absorption available in Aldenmere's Cross. A player who brings this information to the Cravenmoor's Ford council — specifically to Sera Venn, who has been trying to describe this to Wulf Carten without being believed — produces a significant change in the council's dynamics.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Cavan Dalt
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.note: Cavan appears once in the Amber Coast corrections (Cavan Veld, military advisor) — different region, acceptable per naming rules
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: A large, quiet man who does his best work and does not advertise it. The garrison equipment he has been maintaining with insufficient materials is better than it should be. This is not visible to anyone who is not a smith.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Taciturn. Says more in the quality of his work than in his words. Has specific opinions about the garrison's maintenance budget and has decided the garrison's Lord-Marshal does not need to hear them from a blacksmith.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.secret: Has been offered a contract by an Ironmark intermediary to move to Thorngate and establish a smithy there. The pay is better than what he earns at the Cross. He has not accepted and has not refused. Has been sitting on it for three weeks. His hesitation is not political — it is the specific hesitation of a craftsman who has built relationships with his customers and knows that relationships do not transfer with the equipment.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: The blacksmith's apprentice spawn hook entry point. Work-for-work arrangements possible. His Ironmark contract offer is discoverable and is a thread — someone who can explain what Thorngate looks like four years in may help him make the decision.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Osric Fenwall
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.note: Osric appears in the Thornsveld Marches as the ferryman Osric Fenwall — same name, different person, acceptable as a common borderland name. No shared family.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.role: Tallow chandler and Hollow Road waypoint — Crossing Quarter, Aldenmere's Cross
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: Has been deliberately unremarkable for twelve years. Has become genuinely unremarkable, which is different and harder.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Serves customers with appropriate chandler warmth and assesses everyone who enters with the specific attention of someone who has been doing something illegal-adjacent for twelve years and has not been caught.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.secret: His operation predates Garet Denn's by eight years and operates on different principles — professional neutrality, standard Hollow Road ethics. The two operations have a working understanding. He moves information and documents. Garet moves people and cargo. They do not cross into each other's areas. Has been considering whether the current escalation changes this calculation.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: The Hollow Road waypoint entry point for the hollow road runner spawn hook. His sealed packet for Wulf Carten is the entry thread to the Cravenmoor's Ford negotiation channel.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Tev Coldmarsh
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.role: Innkeeper — The Eastern Nail, Crossing Quarter
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: Has been running the first inn travelers reach after crossing the Durnmark for sixteen years. Has the specific quality of someone who knows what tired people need before they know it themselves.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Practical hospitality — no excess warmth, no coldness, the specific efficiency of getting people fed, rested, and out the door for the next crossing.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.secret: Keeps a guest log more detailed than is legally required. Has been keeping it for sixteen years. The guest log does not record anything illegal. It records arrival direction, apparent traveling party composition, cargo type where visible, and departure direction. This is standard inn practice in contested territory. The log is also one of the best travel pattern records in the Borderlands.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.note_on_naming: The Eastern Nail is the inn's name — named for the iron nail that was hammered into the door lintel when it was built, which is still there, which is the oldest iron object in Aldenmere's Cross.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: Primary accommodation in the Crossing Quarter. His guest log is accessible to players who have earned his trust over multiple stays. A player who explains they are tracking specific movement patterns and asks the right questions will receive more than the standard innkeeper's deflection.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Eld Brant
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.role: Tanner — South Shore shed, Aldenmere's Cross
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: The smell of the work has not left him despite being indoors for most of it. The specific build of someone who has been scraping hides since age fourteen.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Knows Garet Denn — they were both tanners, different towns, recognized each other's trade immediately when Garet arrived at the Cross. Does not know what Garet does now beyond 'something that uses the Bleeding Ground regularly.'
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.secret: Has been purchasing small quantities of specific materials from Garet Denn's informal supply chain — materials that cross from Ironmark territory without official documentation, because the documented supply chain for certain chemical components of the tanning process is more expensive than the undocumented one. Is aware this is illegal. Has decided the garrison does not need to know about the economics of his tanning supplies.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: A thread to Garet Denn through professional connection. His supply chain is a minor Hollow Road-adjacent operation that could be leverage or could be a problem depending on who the player is working for.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Bren Mallen
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.role: Food trader — Market Row, Aldenmere's Cross
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: Runs the largest food stall on Market Row. Has the specific knowledge of someone who knows exactly where every item in their inventory came from and what it cost to get it here.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Commercially focused, not politically engaged — or so he presents. His commercial activity requires him to maintain relationships on both sides of the administrative line, which makes him politically engaged whether he admits it or not.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.secret: Has a Saltfen supply relationship that runs through the Ironmark administration — legal, documented, producing food that crosses from Ironmark territory. Three weeks ago the Ironmark administrator in Saltfen told him the supply arrangement would be reviewed 'in light of developing circumstances.' He has not told anyone. The review would cut off approximately 30% of his stock. He has been trying to find alternative supply lines and has not found them.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: Food supply pressure thread. His developing circumstances comment is intelligence about Ironmark intentions before they become official policy. A player who can explain what 'developing circumstances' means in Ironmark administrative language — which requires some knowledge of the Ironmark's internal decision-making — is someone Bren Mallen will pay for.

aldenmeres_cross_civilians.name: Wulf Pendwick
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.physical: Retired from Durn service seventeen years ago, has been at the garrison inn every evening since. This is not waste — he is the most current source of seventeen years of borderland institutional memory available to anyone who buys him a drink.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.disposition: Opinionated about everything that has happened in the Borderlands in the past four decades. Occasionally wrong. More often useful.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.secret: Was at the garrison during the last major Ironmark incursion thirty years ago. Knows what the precursor signs looked like then. Has been watching the current situation for six months and has been comparing notes, mostly to himself, because nobody who would benefit from his comparison is asking.
aldenmeres_cross_civilians.player_interaction: Buy him a drink. He will talk for as long as the drink lasts. His thirty-year memory is the region's oral history and contains two pieces of information that are not in the garrison's written records: the approximate location of the last demonic manifestation in the Bleeding Ground three hundred years ago (inherited knowledge, not personal) and the specific tell that preceded the last Ironmark expansion attempt.

cravenmoores_ford_civilians.name: Tev Holmwick
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.physical: Has been farming the land east of the ford for twenty years. The land does not care about administration changes. He has decided to take his cue from the land.
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.disposition: Stubborn in the specific way of someone who has roots too deep to pull up voluntarily. Is not naive about what is coming. Has decided that if the Ironmark takes the ford he will attempt to continue farming regardless.
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.secret: Has already contacted the Ironmark administration in Weldgate through an informal channel to ask whether existing farm properties would be respected under Ironmark administration. The answer was yes, conditionally. He has not told Torald Marsh this.
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.player_interaction: Represents the portion of Cravenmoor's Ford's population that is quietly exploring accommodation. His Weldgate contact is the thread that leads to what accommodation actually looks like from inside.

cravenmoores_ford_civilians.name: Meven Salthorn
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.role: Reclaimed Path lay elder — Cravenmoor's Ford
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.physical: The oldest person in Cravenmoor's Ford. Has watched three Ironmark settlements get absorbed. Has very specific thoughts about which of the council's three positions is correct.
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.disposition: Does not waste words. At seventy-one in the Borderlands, has concluded that words are expensive and the things that matter are cheaper to demonstrate than to explain.
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.secret: Was a young man in Thorngate when the Ironmark made its first administrative approach thirty years ago — before the Ironmark's current expansion strategy, when the relationship between the powers was different. Knows that the Ironmark has not always been what it is now and that Brekhard specifically changed something in how the Ironmark operates that his predecessors did not do. Has been trying to articulate what changed and has recently concluded: Brekhard's Ironmark does not leave room for the kind of private life that makes accommodation bearable, and this is the thing Thorngate and Saltfen residents do not understand until it is too late.
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.player_interaction: Will share his assessment with a player who asks. His assessment is the most accurate single-sentence description of what distinguishes Brekhard's Ironmark from previous versions — 'they don't leave you a private life' — that exists in the Borderlands.

cravenmoores_ford_civilians.name: Nara Coldwick
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.note: No relation to Bren Coldwick the Grey Contracts sergeant
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.role: Innkeeper — The Ford's Rest, Cravenmoor's Ford
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.physical: Has been running the ford's only inn for eleven years. Has a specific quality of someone who knows exactly what her establishment is worth and does not need anyone to tell her.
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.disposition: Practical, slightly ahead of events in her planning — she has been preparing contingency plans for three different outcomes of the current situation and has specific operational responses ready for each.
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.secret: One of her contingency plans involves burning the inn rather than allowing it to become a garrison for Ironmark soldiers. She has not told anyone this. She has made sure the burn materials are in a specific location. She has also made sure that the guest records are stored separately from the building.
cravenmoores_ford_civilians.player_interaction: The primary accommodation point in Cravenmoor's Ford. Her contingency planning is discoverable and is a specific window into how the ford's residents are thinking about the situation — not with hope, but with preparation.

bleeding_ground_figures.name: Kern Drath
bleeding_ground_figures.role: Ironmark forward scout — operating in the Bleeding Ground
bleeding_ground_figures.physical: Has been running scout routes in the Bleeding Ground for six months. Has the specific quality of someone who has been in territory that is uncomfortable for extended periods and has not fully admitted this to himself.
bleeding_ground_figures.disposition: Professional, following orders, beginning to find the Bleeding Ground more difficult than the first month.
bleeding_ground_figures.secret: Has had the specific dream three times — a sound like grinding stone from below the earth — and has not reported it because it does not fit into the scout report format. Has begun taking slightly longer routes that avoid the center of the Bleeding Ground without acknowledging why.
bleeding_ground_figures.player_interaction: Encounters in the Bleeding Ground. An Ironmark-side thread. His avoidance behavior and the unreported dream are data points in the threshold picture.

bleeding_ground_figures.name: Drev Ashfield
bleeding_ground_figures.role: No-man's-land resident — has been in the Bleeding Ground for eleven years
bleeding_ground_figures.physical: Has been living in the contested strip for longer than anyone can confirm. Shelters in a Hegemony-era foundation that he has roofed with salvaged timber. Does not look like someone who has been living rough for eleven years in a way that suggests either extraordinary constitution or something unusual about his circumstances.
bleeding_ground_figures.disposition: Knows the Bleeding Ground better than anyone except Drevhan Coldbeck and Garet Denn. Does not share this knowledge freely but does not withhold it from people who demonstrate they understand what they are asking.
bleeding_ground_figures.secret: Was not always in the Bleeding Ground. Came here eleven years ago for a reason he does not discuss. Has watched both sides of the border conflict from the only position that belongs to neither. Has been watching the ground change for eleven years and has his own informal system for tracking the change that parallels Drevhan Coldbeck's professional system.
bleeding_ground_figures.player_interaction: Found by players who spend extended time in the Bleeding Ground or who are specifically looking. His eleven years of informal observation is a thread to the threshold picture that does not require finding Drevhan Coldbeck first. His origin — why he came to the Bleeding Ground eleven years ago — is a separate thread that goes somewhere unexpected.

weldgate_figures.name: Lorcan Feld
weldgate_figures.role: Farmer — Weldgate, member of the underground Reclaimed Path congregation
weldgate_figures.physical: Has been farming outside Weldgate for thirty years. The Ironmark's administration changed his administrative relationship to the land but not his relationship to the land. He distinguishes between these two things carefully.
weldgate_figures.disposition: Quiet resistance — not dramatic, not visible, the specific resistance of someone who does not attend Covenant observances and does not stop attending the barn meeting and does not discuss either publicly.
weldgate_figures.secret: The barn meeting has twelve members. He knows that someone in Weldgate has been reporting barn meeting attendance to the Purifier's office. He does not know who. He has been watching for three weeks and has a strong suspicion but not a confirmation.
weldgate_figures.player_interaction: The underground congregation thread. A player who reaches Weldgate and earns his trust enters the congregation network, which connects to Sera Venn's operation at Cravenmoor's Ford and Morna Daelwick's at Aldenmere's Cross.

weldgate_figures.name: Pell Greyvane
weldgate_figures.physical: Competent, organized, not cruel. This last quality is the thing about him that makes the transition slower and in some respects worse than cruelty would have produced.
weldgate_figures.disposition: Genuinely believes the Ironmark's administration is better for these communities than the neglect the Kingdom of Durn provided. Is not wrong that Durn neglected the borderland settlements. Is also producing the conditions for something he cannot see.
weldgate_figures.secret: Has noticed that the Purifier Edric Sorn's program is making people in Weldgate anxious in a way that was not present during the first two months of administration. Has attributed this to normal transition friction. Has not investigated further because the Purifier's program is outside his administrative jurisdiction.
weldgate_figures.player_interaction: The Ironmark's face in a taken town. More reasonable than most players expect. His reasonableness is both genuine and the thing that makes the Ironmark's administration so effectively difficult to resist — you cannot object to reasonable administration on moral grounds until you understand what it produces over time.
